LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SALVADOR CHAVEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION VERSUS DANIEL LEON. COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTON FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO CANNOT ESTABLISH JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT WITHDREW THE FOLLOWING OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE: #6, 13, 14, 15, 16 & 17. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS AS TO DEFENDANT'S REMAINING OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE: #1 SUSTAINED IN PART & OVERRULED IN PART; OVERRULED AS TO #2, 4, 5, 9, 10, 11 & 12; SUSTAINED AS TO #3, 7 & 8. ORDER ON MOTION SIGNED AFTER HEARING (COPY MAILED TO DEFENDANT). JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS THAT SHOW DUE DILIGENCE DOCUMENTS WERE FALSE, WHY DEFENDANT WOULD HAVE A DUTY TO DISCLOSE A $1,000,000 TAX LIABILITY, OR WHETHER THIS LIABILITY WOULD MAKE DEFENDANT'S COMPANY UNHEALTHY FINANCIALLY.
